title: wyred contract v0 — shared sub-shapes ($defs library)
description: Shared sub-shapes for the wyred emit contract v0 (ga019 goldens). This file is a $defs container referenced via relative-file $ref (e.g. "common.defs.json#/$defs/allocation_record"); it is never validated directly against an instance. Created by plan Step 1.1a with the allocation record family (Part C, shared by l1/alloc/records) and the forked_from object (F4). Step 1.1b added the solver-data-path shared shapes: stamp, connector_pinout_row, bound_part_row, wiring_row (producers wyred/emit.py + resolve.py + paths.py; there is no prose — F1).

alloc_entry.description: One realized allocation (schema_l1._alloc_entry_to_json). All six keys are ALWAYS emitted (no emit-only-when-set on this object); locked_by is "<group>@<version>" or null. chosen_by/state are the closed CHOSEN_BY/ALLOC_STATES vocabularies.

lock_group.description: Declared lock group (schema_l1._lock_group_to_json). name/covers/version/snapshot always emitted; owner/sync_point emit-only-when-non-empty (default ""), so they are optional here even though every ga019 golden happens to carry both. covers members are the closed DECISION_CLASSES set. snapshot is F5: null if never locked, else an array of frozen alloc-entry dicts — an EMPTY array is legal at any version (e.g. the external-interface group at version 1 in watchy_v1_reva/_revb).

allocation_record.description: Part C allocation record (schema_l1._allocation_to_json). Embedded in the l1 doc under "allocation" and reused by the alloc-wrapper and records artifacts (Step 1.1b). entries/lock_groups/solver_version are all always emitted (solver_version is a free string).

forked_from_record.description: F4: the series-fork record {series, reason}. This is the OBJECT form used by the l1 and records artifacts (schema_l1 IntentDoc.forked_from, break_lock's escape hatch). NOTE the lifecycle artifact uses forked_from as a bare STRING naming the parent artifact — that is a different shape defined locally in lifecycle.schema.json, not this $def.

stamp.description: The (series, lock-group versions) compatibility stamp shared by the alloc/bom/pinmap/records data paths (resolve.py `alloc["stamp"]`, paths.py `_stamp`). Both keys always emitted. `locks` is a map <lock-group name> -> <version int>; it is {} when no group has fired. A firmware<->board-spin compatibility question is a stamp comparison on the artifact.

connector_pinout_row.description: One external-interface connector-pinout (ICD) row (resolve.py `_connector_rows`, carried by the alloc and records artifacts and — Step 1.1c — the baseline artifact). All five keys always emitted. `net` is the landed net name or null (an unwired connector pin — e.g. a mechanical/shield/ID pin); `locked_by` is the firing connector_pinout lock-group tag "<group>@<version>" or null when the external-interface group has not fired. `pin` is a STRING ("1", "10", "6"), never an integer.

bound_part_row.description: One realized part under a role's bound_parts list (resolve.py: {refdes, part, chosen_by}). All three keys always emitted. `chosen_by` is the closed CHOSEN_BY vocabulary (solver|author) — matching alloc_entry.chosen_by. `part` is the part value/MPN string. Shared by the alloc and records artifacts (bound_parts is a map role-id -> array of these rows).

wiring_row.description: One landed-allocation wiring row (resolve.py `alloc_wiring`): where an allocation entry landed. All five keys always emitted. F9: `node` is the DOTTED "REFDES.PIN" encoding ("U1.A1", "U4.5") — the SAME concept the l2 nets encode as a [refdes, terminal] 2-tuple; the alloc-wiring and pinmap data paths use the dotted string form instead. `net` is the net the node sits on. NOTE: the producer's `self.b.node_net.get(node)` could in principle yield null (an entry whose node is on no net), but NO ga019 golden exhibits that, so this schema keeps `net` a non-null string (tight-to-goldens; see finding F11).
